display.purpose: Net Worth balances and major page headers.

mono.purpose: All monetary values, assets list details, and currency calculations.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Button and navigation hover state transitions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #007AFF; color: #FFFFFF; font-family: Satoshi, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; padding: 10px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 0.2s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.9; }

components.name: Dashboard Card
components.html:
Budgets

Content inside goes here.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #151518; border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08); border-radius: 12px; padding: 24px; color: #F5F5F7; } .ds-card-title { font-family: Satoshi, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 700; margin: 0 0 16px 0; } .ds-card-body { font-family: Satoshi, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; color: #8E8E93; margin: 0; }

narrative.overview: Tidal Finance is a high-precision, developer-tool-styled financial dashboard. The system rejects soft-pastel gradients, round card containers, and arbitrary spacing. Instead, it relies on structural layouts, clear grid lines, and monospaced typography for critical balance numbers.

rules.name: The High-Contrast Accenting Rule
rules.body: The command blue primary color must only be used on interactive components (buttons, links, toggles) or state markers. It must never be used for purely decorative highlights.

rules.name: The Border Grid Depth Rule
rules.body: Components exist on the same flat layer. Overlapping or floating components (like dropdown lists or modal dialogs) must be bounded by a solid 1px border instead of a diffuse ambient shadow.

dos: Do display every dollar and rupee amount using the monospaced Geist Mono font family.
Do split screens using strict borders (1px) to divide modules cleanly.
Do enforce WCAG AA (>= 4.5:1) color contrast on all form inputs and button states.
donts: Don't nest card components inside other cards.
Don't use thick left-side accent color stripes for alert messages or badge lists.
Don't add drop shadows for hover effects; use border color updates or opacity changes.
